entries.value: Editor display language, saved per user.
The display language is saved per user in EditorPrefs, not written to project assets. Resolution order: scope override → global setting → system language → each scope's default.
Re-scan l10n manifests and reload all translation tables.
Check missing keys, placeholder numbers, and untranslated entries. Results go to the Console.
Reload re-scans manifests and reloads tables. Validate checks keys, placeholders, and fallbacks across locales.
Validation found {0} errors · {1} warnings. See the Console.
The display language used when a scope has no override.
When unset, follows the system language {0} (or each scope's default if unsupported).
The system language couldn't be detected, so unset uses each scope's default.
When unset, uses each scope's default language.
When the global setting is unset, match the OS language and fall back to each scope's default if there is no matching translation.
No l10n manifest found. Add a manifest to set a display language per scope.
The display language applied to this scope only.
This scope has no available locale. Check the manifest's locales.
Searches the requested locale, its parents, then defaultLocale, and shows the first language that has a translation.
Requested {0} is outside the loaded catalogs.
Click to select the manifest asset.
Register the bundled skills into .claude/skills and .agents/skills via symlinks so agents like Claude Code can use them.
Keep translation quality with terminology, style, per-language notes, and a validation script.

Scaffold the setup (2 assemblies) that adds UnityEditorLocalization to your existing editor extension as an optional dependency. It runs in a single language without the package, and lights up multi-language UI and a locale switcher once the package is installed.
Register into ~/.claude/skills and ~/.agents/skills in your home.
